title: Expand database provider support to Cosmos DB, MongoDB, and AWS/GCP equivalents
body:
## User Story

As a platform engineer, I want Concoction to support additional cloud and document databases so that teams can discover schemas and generate synthetic data for more of their production data estate.

## Business Requirements

- Expand beyond SQLite/PostgreSQL to cover document and cloud-native database platforms.
- Prioritize Azure Cosmos DB and MongoDB support.
- Define AWS and GCP equivalents that should be supported in the same roadmap slice.
- Preserve Concoction's deterministic generation and validation workflow across new providers.

## Technical Requirements

- Evaluate provider adapters for Cosmos DB (including Mongo-compatible/document APIs where appropriate), MongoDB, and AWS/GCP equivalents such as DynamoDB and Firestore/Spanner based on the target data model.
- Extend discovery abstractions so non-relational providers can describe collections/documents, nested fields, indexes, partition keys, and relationship hints in the canonical metadata model.
- Identify provider-specific profiling, sampling, and export constraints for document/NoSQL engines.
- Define DI-registered infrastructure adapters and any new application/domain abstractions needed to keep hexagonal boundaries clean.
- Document security and privacy constraints for connecting to managed cloud databases.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A supported-provider roadmap identifies the initial Azure, AWS, GCP, and MongoDB targets.
- [ ] Canonical metadata gaps for document/NoSQL providers are documented.
- [ ] Discovery/profiling adapter design exists for the first wave of new providers.
- [ ] Provider-specific auth/connection requirements are documented.
- [ ] Test strategy covers representative cloud/document database fixtures or sandboxes.
- [ ] Follow-up implementation issues can be split by provider without losing shared architecture work.
